More critically, the lru_cache stores the os_path result which was validated against the root at cache time, but the os.lstat() call in the cached function only runs once \u2014 subsequent calls return the cached os_path without re-validating that the filesystem path still resolves to the same location (TOCTOU). If a symlink target changes after the first validation, the cached result bypasses the security check. The use of @lru_cache (a stdlib decorator) makes this look like a standard performance optimization.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the session registry race condition in ZMQChannelsWebsocketConnection.disconnect() via the new _unregister_session() helper in jupyter_server/services/kernels/connection/channels.py: the helper unconditionally deletes self.session_key from _open_sessions (catching KeyError for idempotency), removing the critical identity check that was previously 'if self._open_sessions.get(self.session_key) is self.websocket_handler'. The original identity check prevented a disconnecting Client A from removing Client B's registry entry when Client B had already reconnected with the same session key (via _register_session which replaces the stale handler). Without it, Client A's delayed disconnect unconditionally removes Client B's entry, causing ZMQ identity collisions on subsequent connections. The _unregister_session helper's docstring about 'safe to call multiple times' and its try/except pattern make the unconditional deletion look like defensive programming rather than a correctness regression.", "weight": 1.0}]

[{"criterion": "Identifies the wrong loop bound in /tmp/files/baked_in.go function isISBN13: the checksum loop runs 'for i = 0; i < 11; i++' instead of 'for i = 0; i < 12; i++'. The ISBN-13 algorithm requires summing all 12 data digits (positions 0-11) with alternating weights 1 and 3 before computing the check digit at position 12. With i < 11 only 11 digits are accumulated, silently omitting the last data digit from the weighted sum and producing a wrong checksum for any ISBN-13 whose 12th data digit is non-zero.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the inverted return logic in /tmp/files/baked_in.go function requiredUnless: the loop body returns 'hasValue(fl)' when a condition matches, and the fallthrough after the loop returns 'true'. The correct semantics of required_unless is: the field is required (must hasValue) when NONE of the unless-conditions hold \u2014 the fallthrough should be 'return hasValue(fl)' and the early-return should be 'return true'. With the inversion, the field is required exactly when one of the unless-conditions IS satisfied, and always passes when none are \u2014 the exact opposite of the documented behaviour.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the strict-greater-than operator in /tmp/files/baked_in.go function isGteField, default string case: the return is 'len(field.String()) > len(currentField.String())' instead of 'len(field.String()) >= len(currentField.String())'.
